zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Catherine Fenton於1548年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 沃特福德利斯莫時，她的父親Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire46歲，母親Lady Cecily Beaumont44歲。她於1575年1月11日與Sir William Parsons在英國結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1618年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾格亞特 米爾頓享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fenton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

variants.name: Hunt
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a hunter, from Middle English hunte ‘hunter, huntsman’ (Old English hunta). The term was used not only of the hunting on horseback of game such as stags and wild boars, which in the Middle Ages was a pursuit restricted to the ranks of the nobility, but also to much humbler forms of pursuit such as bird catching and poaching for food. The word seems also to have been used as an Old English personal name and to have survived into the Middle Ages as an occasional personal name. Compare Huntington and Huntley .
Irish: adopted for various Irish surnames containing or thought to contain the Gaelic element fiadhach ‘hunt’; for example Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) and Ó Fiachna (see Fenton ).
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Hundt  .

variants.name: Fantom
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English fantom, fainton ‘apparition, ghost’. There has been some confusion with Fenton .

variants.name: Finton
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Fenton  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Catherine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Catherine Fenton was born in 1548, in Lismore, County Waterford, Ireland, her father, Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire, was 46 and her mother, Lady Cecily Beaumont, was 44. She married Sir William Parsons on 11 January 1575, in England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. She died about 1618, in Great Milton, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Catherine Fenton est née en 1548, à Lismore, County Waterford, Irlande, son père, Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Lady Cecily Beaumont, avait 44 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Parsons le 11 janvier 1575, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1618, à Great Milton, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Catherine Fentonが1548年にアイルランドコウントイ ワテルフォードリスモーで生まれた時，父Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshireは46歳，母Lady Cecily Beaumontは44歳であった。1575年1月11日にイギリスでSir William Parsonsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1618年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアグレアト ミルトンで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Catherine Fenton(이)가 1548년 아일랜드 카운티 왓르퍼드 리스모어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Cecily Beaumont(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 1575년 1월 11일 영국에서 Sir William Parsons(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1618년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 그리트 미턴에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir William Parsons em 11 de janeiro de 1575, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Catherine Fenton родилась в 1548 г. в местности лисмор, коунти вэитирфорд, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Cecily Beaumont, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Parsons 11 января 1575 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Она умерла около 1618 г. в местности грит милтон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год.
